# Markout

> A Crystal library for converting HTML to clean, readable Markdown. Optimized for LLM data preparation, RAG pipelines, and content extraction. 25-40x faster than Python alternatives.

Markout converts HTML documents into well-formatted Markdown using a handler registry pattern. Each HTML element type has a dedicated handler class that processes it. The library automatically strips non-content elements (scripts, styles, navigation) making output ideal for LLM consumption.

## Quick Start

```crystal
require "markout"

# Basic conversion
markdown = Markout.convert("<h1>Title</h1><p>Content</p>")
# => "# Title\n\nContent"

# With options
options = Markout::Options.new
options.heading_style = Markout::Options::HeadingStyle::Setext
markdown = Markout.convert(html, options)

# Reusable converter
converter = Markout::Converter.new(options)
result = converter.convert(html)
```

## Architecture

The conversion flow:
1. HTML is parsed into a DOM tree using the Lexbor library
2. `Converter` recursively walks the tree, delegating to appropriate handlers
3. `Context` tracks state during conversion (nesting depth, list state, references)
4. Each handler returns the Markdown string for its element

Key components:
- `src/markout.cr` - Public API entry point with `convert` overloads
- `src/markout/options.cr` - `Options` class with all configuration settings
- `src/markout/context.cr` - `Context` class for tracking conversion state
- `src/markout/converter.cr` - `Converter` class that orchestrates the conversion
- `src/markout/handler.cr` - `Handler` module (abstract interface) and `Handlers` registry

## Handler Pattern

Handlers implement the `Handler` module with two methods:
- `handles : Array(Symbol)` - declares which HTML tags this handler processes
- `convert(node, ctx, converter) : String` - performs the conversion

Example handler:

```crystal
module Markout::Handlers
  class HeadingsHandler
    include Handler

    def handles : Array(Symbol)
      [:h1, :h2, :h3, :h4, :h5, :h6]
    end

    def convert(node : Lexbor::Node, ctx : Context, converter : Converter) : String
      level = heading_level(node)
      text = converter.process_children(node, ctx).strip
      "#" * level + " #{text}"
    end
  end

  Handlers.register(HeadingsHandler.new)
end
```

Available handlers in `src/markout/_handlers/`:
- `text.cr` - Text nodes (`:_text`)
- `headings.cr` - H1-H6 elements
- `paragraphs.cr` - P tags and BR line breaks
- `emphasis.cr` - strong, b, em, i formatting
- `links.cr` - Anchor tags with inline/referenced styles
- `images.cr` - Image tags
- `code.cr` - Inline code and fenced code blocks
- `horizontal_rules.cr` - HR elements
- `lists.cr` - UL, OL, LI elements with nesting support
- `blockquotes.cr` - Blockquote elements with nesting
- `tables.cr` - Table, TR, TH, TD elements
- `strikethrough.cr` - DEL, S, STRIKE elements
- `strip.cr` - Removes non-content elements (script, style, nav, aside, header, footer, form elements)

## Configuration Options

All options are properties on `Markout::Options`:

```crystal
# Heading style
property heading_style : HeadingStyle = HeadingStyle::ATX  # or Setext

# List formatting
property bullet_char : Char = '-'      # or '*'
property list_indent : Int32 = 2

# Emphasis formatting
property emphasis_char : Char = '*'    # or '_'
property strong_char : String = "**"   # or "__"

# Code formatting
property code_fence : String = "```"   # or "~~~"

# Horizontal rules
property hr_style : String = "---"     # or "***" or "___"

# Link handling
property link_style : LinkStyle = LinkStyle::Inline  # or Referenced
property autolinks : Bool = true       # Use <url> when text matches href
property ignore_links : Bool = false   # Strip link formatting, keep text
property default_link_title : Bool = false

# Image handling
property ignore_images : Bool = false
property images_as_html : Bool = false  # Keep <img> as raw HTML

# Table handling
property ignore_tables : Bool = false
property tables_as_html : Bool = false  # Keep tables as raw HTML

# Content filtering
property strip_tags : Array(String) = [] of String
property convert_only : Array(String)? = nil

# Whitespace
property strip_document : Bool = true   # Trim leading/trailing whitespace
property newline_style : NewlineStyle = NewlineStyle::Spaces  # or Backslash
property wrap : Bool = false
property wrap_width : Int32 = 80
property single_line_break : Bool = false
```

## Adding a New Handler

1. Create a file in `src/markout/_handlers/`
2. Define a class inside `module Markout::Handlers` that includes `Handler`
3. Implement `handles` returning array of tag symbols
4. Implement `convert` method
5. Call `Handlers.register(YourHandler.new)` at the bottom

Template:

```crystal
module Markout::Handlers
  class MyHandler
    include Handler

    def handles : Array(Symbol)
      [:mytag]
    end

    def convert(node : Lexbor::Node, ctx : Context, converter : Converter) : String
      # Access child content
      content = converter.process_children(node, ctx)

      # Access options
      converter.options.some_option

      # Access/modify context state
      ctx.some_state

      # Return markdown string
      "formatted #{content}"
    end
  end

  Handlers.register(MyHandler.new)
end
```

## Context State

The `Context` class tracks:
- `blockquote_depth : Int32` - Current blockquote nesting level
- `in_code_block? : Bool` - Whether inside a pre/code block
- `in_link? : Bool` - Whether inside a link
- `list_depth : Int32` - Current list nesting depth
- `current_list_type : Symbol?` - :ul or :ol
- `references : Hash(Int32, Reference)` - For reference-style links

Methods:
- `enter_list(type, start_index)` / `exit_list` - Manage list state
- `next_list_index : Int32` - Get and increment ordered list counter
- `push_blockquote` / `pop_blockquote` - Manage blockquote depth
- `add_reference(url, title) : Int32` - Add reference link, returns ID

## Build Commands

```bash
shards install          # Install dependencies
crystal spec            # Run tests
crystal tool format     # Format code
bin/ameba               # Run linter
crystal docs            # Generate documentation
```

## Performance Characteristics

Benchmarks vs Python markdownify on 210KB Wikipedia HTML:
- Markout: 2.2ms, 47KB output
- markdownify: 61ms, 65KB output
- Result: 28x faster, 28% smaller output

The smaller output is due to the strip handler removing navigation, sidebars, and UI elements that aren't article content.

## Code Style

- 2-space indentation
- UTF-8 encoding, LF line endings
- Use `property?` for boolean properties (Crystal convention)
- Documentation comments on all public types and methods
- Crystal >= 1.10.0 required
